meta.title: From Bean to Brew
meta.subtitle: Discover how carefully sourced coffee beans become the perfect cup through roasting, grinding, and brewing.

hero.title: From Bean to Brew
hero.subtitle: Follow the journey of carefully sourced coffee beans—from world-renowned farms to the perfect cup brewed with precision and passion.

timeline.title: Coffee Beans Harvested
timeline.description: Premium Arabica beans are carefully handpicked from high-altitude farms in Brazil for their balanced sweetness and rich flavour.

timeline.title: Beans Arrive at the Roastery
timeline.description: Fresh green beans are inspected, sorted, and prepared before entering the roasting process.

timeline.title: Roasted to Perfection
timeline.description: The beans are roasted at carefully controlled temperatures to unlock their natural aroma and flavour profile.

timeline.title: Ground for Brewing
timeline.description: Freshly roasted beans are ground to match the selected brewing method for optimal extraction.

timeline.title: Brew the Perfect Cup
timeline.description: Hot or cold brewing extracts the coffee's unique flavours, creating a smooth and aromatic cup ready to enjoy.

details.title: Brazil Santos
details.summary: Smooth, nutty and chocolatey. A balanced coffee that works beautifully for espresso and milk-based drinks.
details.fullContent: Brazil Santos is one of the world's most popular Arabica coffees. It offers low acidity, a creamy body and sweet notes of chocolate and roasted nuts, making it an excellent everyday coffee.

details.title: Ethiopia Yirgacheffe
details.summary: Bright floral aromas with citrus and berry notes. Perfect for pour-over coffee lovers.
details.fullContent: Grown at high altitudes, Ethiopian Yirgacheffe beans produce delicate floral aromas with hints of jasmine, lemon and berries. Its vibrant acidity makes every cup refreshing and complex.

details.title: Colombia Supremo
details.summary: Balanced sweetness with caramel and red fruit flavours. A versatile choice for any brew method.
details.fullContent: Colombia Supremo beans are known for their smooth body and balanced flavour. Expect caramel sweetness, subtle fruit notes and a clean finish that's enjoyable from espresso to filter coffee.

details.title: Guatemala Antigua
details.summary: Rich cocoa flavours with gentle spice and a full-bodied finish.
details.fullContent: Cultivated in volcanic soil, Guatemala Antigua beans develop deep cocoa notes with hints of spice and brown sugar. Their rich body makes them ideal for bold black coffee.

comparison.title: Hot Brew vs Cold Brew
comparison.description: Slide to compare two popular brewing methods and discover which coffee suits your taste.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.description: Learn more about coffee beans, brewing methods, and how to enjoy the perfect cup.

items.question: Why do coffee beans from different countries taste different?
items.answer: Climate, altitude, soil, and processing methods all influence a coffee bean's flavour. That's why beans from Brazil, Ethiopia, Colombia, and Guatemala each have their own unique taste profile.

items.question: Should coffee beans be ground fresh?
items.answer: Yes. Grinding coffee just before brewing preserves its natural aroma and essential oils, resulting in a fresher and more flavourful cup.

items.question: What is the ideal water temperature for brewing coffee?
items.answer: Most specialty coffee is best brewed between 90°C and 96°C. Water that's too hot can create bitterness, while cooler water may under-extract the coffee.

items.question: How should I store coffee beans?
items.answer: Keep coffee beans in an airtight container away from heat, light, and moisture. Avoid storing them in the refrigerator to preserve their freshness and flavour.
